title: Shipwright Deploy Profile
description: Declarative descriptor for a Shipwright deploy target. Encodes universal rollback discipline + adjacent deploy concerns (auth, environments, smoke test, migrations) for one target. See shared/profiles/deploy-profile-schema.md for field semantics and the 'Adding a New Target' checklist.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema for IDE autocompletion.

profile_schema_version.description: Schema version this profile targets. v1.0 is the only valid value today.

target_id.description: Stable, lowercase, hyphen-separated identifier. MUST equal the profile filename without extension.

target_kind.description: Coarse classification. Recommended values: 'paas-cloud' (Jelastic, Heroku-style), 'edge-platform' (Vercel, Cloudflare Pages), 'self-hosted-vm' (Docker Compose on VPS, bare metal). Freeform string — not a strict enum, so future categories don't require a schema bump.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the target. For stubs, describe scope, source-of-information, and known gaps.

implementation_status.description: 'shipped' means a code-backed runtime client exists in this repo. 'stub' means the profile is declarative-only (reference / future-implementation skeleton).

confidence.description: How well-grounded the profile fields are. 'verified' = backed by working implementation. 'documented' = derived from official target docs. 'inferred' = derived from general industry patterns. Required for ALL profiles. Schema rules below enforce: shipped→verified, stub→{documented|inferred}.

client.description: When implementation_status=shipped, MUST be a non-null object pointing at the runtime client. When stub, MUST be null.

entrypoint.description: Repo-relative path to the client script. MUST NOT contain '..' or be an absolute path. Validator's --strict mode resolves this against repo_root and verifies the file exists.

auth.description: Authentication requirements. Profiles describe ENV variable NAMES — never literal secret values. Validator enforces no duplicate names within or across the two lists.

environments.description: Per-environment URL pattern + confirmation policy. Schema requires 'dev' and 'prod' keys. Additional keys matching '^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$' (preview, staging, uat, etc.) are accepted via patternProperties and validate against the same env-shape — extending without a schema bump. Keys NOT matching the regex (e.g. 'Staging' uppercase, 'pre/view') are rejected via additionalProperties:false. JSON-Schema Draft 2020-12 semantics: patternProperties matches take precedence over additionalProperties — this is intentional and verified against the validator's regression tests (jsonschema 4.26+).

url_pattern.description: URL template with placeholders like {project} or {domain}.

confirmation.description: Whether deployment to this environment requires user confirmation.

pre_deploy_safety.description: How a rollback point is established before each deploy. Strategy values are recommended-not-enum strings.

dev_strategy.description: Recommended values: 'none' (DEV is auto-fixable), 'git-tag' (post-fact revert), 'image-snapshot' (registry-immutable tag), 'atomic-immutable' (deploy is intrinsically preserved).

backup_naming_pattern.description: Naming convention for backup artifacts (e.g. '{prod_env}-backup'). null when not applicable (atomic-immutable targets).

test_gate.description: Whether and how Shipwright requires test results before deploy.

policy.description: 'strict' blocks deploy on test failure. 'warn' logs but continues. 'skip' bypasses gate.

results_source.description: Path (relative to project root) of the test-results JSON file consumed by the gate.

migrations.description: Database / schema migration support. Conditional schema: when supported=false, strategy fields are FORBIDDEN (avoids 'n/a' string-as-enum-value).

verify_after_apply.description: Whether the runtime runs verifier scripts after applying migrations (e.g. -- VERIFY: comments).

rollback.description: Universal rollback discipline encoded as required mechanism fields. The act of populating these (with non-empty values) IS the discipline assertion — no separate boolean block. See plugins/shipwright-deploy/skills/deploy/references/rollback-discipline.md for the discipline statements.

auto_trigger.description: Recommended: 'smoke-test-fail', 'verify-fail', 'off'. 'off' is honest for targets with no built-in auto-rollback (e.g. Vercel).

manual_procedure_reference.description: Documentation-only freeform reference. May be a CLI command, doc URL, or short prose. NEVER executed by Shipwright runtime — readers/operators run it themselves.

data_rollback_strategy.description: How the target handles data-tier rollback (separate from app-tier). Recommended: 'down-migration', 'backup-restore', 'none-app-only', 'manual'. Pattern 1 (Revertable Deploys) explicitly covers application-tier vs data-tier distinction in the discipline doc.

known_gaps.description: Optional honest-limitations list. Especially valuable for stubs: e.g. 'custom aliases not auto-rolled', 'no built-in health-check rollback'. Replaces buried prose in description.

name.description: ENV variable NAME (e.g. 'JELASTIC_TOKEN'). Profiles MUST NOT contain literal secret values.
